titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Würzburg Univ. M.p.th.f.67
VL11A
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Mc Lc Jo

listBibl.bibl: Digitized black and white microfilm on CD in ITSEE, supplied by Würzburg University.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John in 2008.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 155v-192v. The manuscript is missing John 18:35 to the end. There are two forms of the letter u, one more like a v or y. There is some inconsistency in the abbreviations: 'eius' can appear as 'ei(u)s', 'ei(us)' or 'eius', and autem as 'au(tem)', 'aut(em)' or 'aute(m)'. 'est' is usually abbreviated to 'e(st)', and is normally divided from 'pot' in 'potest'. 'dix' can be an abbreviation for 'dixi' or 'dixit'.

Word division is initially by interpunction, although this becomes less frequent during the course of John. It is sometimes idiosyncratic, and there are numerous instances of single letters being left at the beginning or end of a line: this syllabification has been taken as an indiciation of the Breton origin of the manuscript. There are a few corrections, but many errors, especially of omission due to eyeskip, have not been corrected.

The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation, digraphs/digraphs and abbreviations. Different forms of 'u' have not been recorded, nor has the interpunction. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: Luke ends in the middle of this column. The Incipit to John is double line spaced and almost illegible; catalogue notes "Die Einleitung is fast nicht mehr zu lesen".
This is not the usual abbreviation for 'eum', and the copyist appears to have used a 'v' or 'y' rather than a 'u'.
Word division 'nos set', with interpunction.
'ne' is written tucked underneath the line.
There is a line above the 'a' of 'loquar'.
There is another character before the 'r' of 'respondit'; either a 't' which is partially covered by the 'r', or the '7'-shaped symbol for 'et'.
There appear to be erased characters at the end of this line.
This space is probably an erased word.
The word division is unclear at the end of this line.
There is a hole in the parchment in the middle of 'uenerit'.
There is a hole in the parchment after 'eum'.
sic
There is a hole in the parchment between 'fa' and 'ci'.
sic
Possibly a correction from 'facit' to 'fecit'.
There is no abbreviation mark for 'ei(u)s' visible.
sic
'loquitur' may have been corrected from 'loquiatur'.
sic
'eius' has a superline even though it is not abbreviated.
There is an illegible gloss in the bottom margin of ten letters, which does not appear to be Latin.
sic
The order of the corrections is unclear.
sic
'est' has a superline even though it does not appear to be an abbreviation.
Quire signature XXIII at foot of page.
This may be a correction from 'faciat' to 'faciet'.
'eum' appears to have a superline above it, although it is not an abbreviation.
'pacem' appears to have a superline, even though it is not an abbreviation.
The word division is 'are fiant'.
There is no overline abbreviation for 'eius' visible.
There is a hole in the parchment in the middle of 'fecissem'.
The word is divided 'hab iero'.
There is a hole in the parchment.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
There appears to be a superline on 'est', even though there is no abbreviation.
Quire signature XXIIII at foot of page. The manuscript breaks off here.
